description:
# Why

bl-228d unified bl's two hand-maintained `.balls/` runtime-path lists into one
canonical table (`runtime_paths::RUNTIME_PATHS`, derived by `backstop_paths()`
and `gitignore_paths()`). One enumeration of those same paths was deliberately
left behind, out of that ball's scope: `review_safety::runtime_in_squash_error`
builds its user-facing help text with a hardcoded literal —

    The work branch has tracked a `.balls/` subpath (`local`, `tasks`,
    `worktree`, or `code-refs`) ...

It is already stale: bl-c439 added `.balls/state-repo` to the backstop set, but
this string was never updated, so a state-repo rejection prints help text whose
example list omits `state-repo`. More to the point, it is the exact "manual
sync, standing invitation to drift" pattern bl-228d set out to eliminate — just
expressed in prose instead of a `const`. The fix that ball shipped is only
complete once this last enumeration also derives from the table.

This is cosmetic, not a behavior bug: the error already interpolates the actual
offending `{list}` of paths that triggered the rejection; the stale literal is
only the explanatory parenthetical.

# Scope

- `review_safety::runtime_in_squash_error`: build the parenthetical example
  list from `runtime_paths::backstop_paths()` instead of the literal.
- Note for the implementer: `backstop_paths()` returns fully-qualified paths
  (`.balls/local`, ...), while the current prose reads "a `.balls/` subpath
  (`local`, ...)" with the prefix factored into the sentence. Either strip the
  `.balls/` prefix when rendering, or rephrase the sentence — don't leave a
  doubled `.balls/.balls/local`. Every backstop path is a `.balls/` subpath
  (`.balls-worktrees` is gitignore-only, backstop:false), so the framing holds.
- `runtime_in_squash_error_pluralizes_correctly` should still pass; extend it
  if the table-derivation needs coverage for the 100% gate.

# Out of scope

The rejection logic itself — it is already table-derived (`commit_touches_runtime`
via `backstop_paths()`) and correct. This ball is only the human-readable string.

# Origin

Flagged in the bl-228d close-out (2026-05-20): the refactor unified the two path
*lists* but left one path *enumeration* sitting in an error string. Small,
independently shippable.
title: runtime_in_squash_error: derive its path list from the runtime_paths table
